name: Hot Tamale Pie
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon Favorite sauteeing medium (wine, broth, or water)
1 medium Yellow bell pepper, or red or green, seeded and finely chopped
16 ounces pinto beans, canned, rinsed and drained
1 ear Corn, kernels cut off OR 3/4 cup Frozen corn, thawed
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the favorite sauteeing medium in a large frying pan over medium-high heat.
2. Add the onion, bell pepper, and garlic; cook until softened, 6 to 8 minutes.
3. Remove from the heat and stir in the tomato sauce, pinto beans, corn (fresh or thawed), chili powder, cumin, salt, and cayenne. Pour into an 8 x 8 glass baking dish.
4. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees.
5. In a large saucepan, boil the water; add the cornmeal, lemon juice, mustard, and salt. Stir until mixed.
6. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat, then immediately reduce the heat to low and simmer, stirring often, until thickened, 4 to 5 minutes.
7. Spread the cooked cornmeal over the bean mixture. Bake for 30 minutes. Cool for 10 minutes before serving.
